# Source Card: Allogeneic Hematopoietic Cell Transplantation for the Treatment of Severe Aplastic Anemia — ASTCT Evidence-Based Guidelines

## Summary
This 2024 ASTCT Practice Guideline (GRADE methodology, combined pediatric and adult transplant panel) establishes transplant-specific consensus recommendations for allogeneic HCT in severe aplastic anemia (SAA). For the pediatric CDS module it supplies qualitative, guideline-anchored facts — bone marrow as preferred graft source, rabbit over horse ATG, cyclophosphamide-ATG vs fludarabine conditioning by risk/donor, and prioritization of MUD/haploidentical HCT over immunosuppressive therapy for children lacking a matched related donor. Its only explicit numeric thresholds are chronological-age eligibility boundaries (the historical 40-year cutoff, explicitly rejected, replaced by an "up to 50 yr or beyond" framing); these are adult-facing eligibility criteria, not pediatric reference intervals or lab cutoffs.

- (ev_002) Rabbit ATG over horse ATG — locator: Abstract / ATG recommendation (S, L) — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:12b939e9b3223887e62a589d2f111c730c7e8b0c5bbec3e97ce0ca5e1249c419]"

- (ev_006) Prioritize MUD/haplo HCT over IST when no MRD (children + adults) — locator: Abstract / donor-selection recommendation (C, L) — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:36a2b535bc48b036c606107dafc50c3f7b94bd79032a3311691810bd48079fc1]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population scope: severe aplastic anemia patients undergoing allogeneic HCT — the panel explicitly combined pediatric and adult transplant physicians and issues pediatric/AYA-specific statements (upfront MRD-HCT; Cy-ATG conditioning for pediatric/AYA MRD-HCT).
- Assay/method dependence: none of the material recommendations are laboratory-assay thresholds; the only numeric values are chronological-age eligibility boundaries (method-independent).
- Lifecycle: effective 2024-09 (Transplant Cell Ther 2024;30(12):1155-1170); guideline version = ASTCT SAA HCT guideline 2024; supersedes the historical 40-year upfront-HCT age cutoff convention. No stated retirement date.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Decision-boundary triggers relevant to a CDS: (a) age eligibility for upfront HCT (historical 40-year cutoff rejected; upper framing "up to 50 yr or beyond") — an adult-facing boundary; (b) absence of a matched related donor as the branch point to prioritize MUD/haplo HCT over immunosuppressive therapy in children and adults. These are transplant-eligibility decision points, not diagnostic red-flags. No pediatric CBC/lab referral threshold is defined here.

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP (numeric): The guideline provides NO numeric ATG dose, no graft cell-dose (CD34/nucleated-cell) threshold, and no numeric hematologic cutoff defining "severe cytopenias" — these thresholds are not locatable in this source and are recorded as gaps, not asserted.
- Source-fidelity caveat: the primary full text is paywalled; abstract quotes (ev_001, ev_002, ev_003, ev_005, ev_006) are verbatim from PubMed (primary), while the graded recommendation-statement wording and the "up to 50 yr" numeric framing (ev_004) were captured from the Guideline Central digest of the primary guideline — flagged in threshold.passage_locator and trust.reliability_notes. First live confirmation against the primary PDF remains outstanding.
- Scope conflict/coexistence: this HCT-specific guideline coexists with broader SAA management consensus statements (e.g., the 2024 Blood Advances "Consensus recommendations for severe aplastic anemia," Blood Adv 8(21):5719) that also cover non-transplant IST pathways; no numeric conflict was identified in the accessed material and none is asserted. Any apparent divergence in first-line IST-vs-HCT sequencing should be preserved as a distinct-source difference, not averaged.
- This card is a PROPOSAL for the CDS converter describing what the ASTCT guideline states; it issues no diagnosis, treatment, dosing, or transfusion directive.
